description: Identifies S3 buckets with versioning and growing noncurrent versions, incomplete multipart uploads, storage-class mismatch, or lifecycle governance gaps while protecting retention and compliance requirements.

cost.pricing_source: Static fallback estimate. Prefer AWS Pricing MCP, AWS Pricing API, S3 Storage Lens, or cost_report region-specific pricing when available.
cost.note: Noncurrent versions are billed as stored objects. Lifecycle expiration permanently deletes versions; storage-class transitions can introduce retrieval and minimum-duration charges.

severity_rules.name: versioning_no_noncurrent_lifecycle
severity_rules.condition: versioning_status == 'Enabled' AND lifecycle_has_noncurrent_expiration == false AND noncurrent_version_count_slope > threshold AND no retention blocker evidence
severity_rules.description: Versioning is enabled and noncurrent versions are growing. Add a noncurrent lifecycle policy only after validating restore, Object Lock, replication, legal hold, backup, and compliance requirements.

severity_rules.name: incomplete_multipart_upload_accumulation
severity_rules.condition: incomplete_multipart_uploads_age_days >= incomplete_multipart_upload_age_days_min AND no abort_incomplete_multipart_upload rule
severity_rules.description: Incomplete multipart uploads may be accumulating storage cost. Add abort rules when upload workflows support it.

severity_rules.name: retention_blocker_present
severity_rules.condition: object_lock_enabled == true OR legal_hold == true OR compliance_data == true OR replication_policy_requires_versions == true
severity_rules.description: Retention or replication evidence exists. Do not expire noncurrent versions until the retention policy allows it.

severity_rules.name: storage_class_transition_candidate
severity_rules.description: Storage class transitions may reduce cost, but retrieval, monitoring, request, and minimum-duration charges must be modeled.

severity_rules.name: missing_lifecycle_governance_tags
severity_rules.condition: missing Owner OR DataClass OR RetentionDays OR CostCenter tags
severity_rules.description: Missing lifecycle governance tags make safe retention decisions difficult.

severity_rules.name: request_amplification_or_cache_miss
severity_rules.condition: GET_OR_HEAD_request_rate_material == true AND upstream_requests_per_invocation is high_or_rising AND cache_hit_rate is missing_or_low
severity_rules.description: S3 request cost may be driven by repeated upstream reads rather than storage lifecycle. Attribute the caller and recommend caching or request coalescing before lifecycle-only remediation.

validation_before_change: Check Object Lock, legal hold, compliance, backup, replication, and restore requirements.
Check data classification and owner approval.
Model storage class transition retrieval and minimum-duration charges.
Attribute high request rates to callers before changing lifecycle or storage class.
Apply lifecycle changes gradually where risk is high.
reporting: Separate noncurrent expiration, multipart abort, and storage-class transition savings.
Separate request-reduction/cache savings from storage lifecycle savings.
Use region-specific pricing and storage class line items when available.
Report confidence based on retention evidence and cost line-item quality.
